---
title: "austraits"
author: "Fonti Kar, Daniel Falster"
date: "2023-01-05"
output: 
  rmarkdown::html_vignette:
  toc: yes
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{austraits}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---



The `austraits` package is designed to aid users in accessing data from [AusTraits](https://zenodo.org/record/5112001), a curated plant trait database for the Australian flora. This package contains several core functions to explore, wrangle and visualise data. Below we have prepared a short vignette illustrating these. 

The structure of AusTraits has been significantly updated with version 4.0.0. As such, the syntax and output of some functions will differ depending on what version of AusTraits is loaded. The vignette below is written for version 4.0.0 and newer. The same vignette for versions 3.0.2 and older can be found on our website under the Documentation tab

## Getting started
`austraits` is still under development. To install the current version from GitHub: 


```r
#install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("traitecoevo/austraits", dependencies = TRUE, upgrade = "ask")

# Load the austraits package
library(austraits)
```

## AusTraits is version controlled

AusTraits is always updating and ingesting new data. Each release is tracked and provided with a unique version number. You can check out different versions and their associated DOI of AusTraits by using: 

```r
get_versions(path = "data/austraits")
```

```
#> # A tibble: 6 × 3
#>   date       version doi                   
#>   <chr>      <chr>   <chr>                 
#> 1 2022-11-27 4.0.0   10.5281/zenodo.7368074
#> 2 2021-07-14 3.0.2   10.5281/zenodo.5112001
#> 3 2021-07-14 3.0.1   10.5281/zenodo.5099552
#> 4 2021-07-14 3.0.0   10.5281/zenodo.5099545
#> 5 2020-12-11 2.1.0   10.5281/zenodo.4316550
#> 6 2020-07-08 2.0.0   10.5281/zenodo.4316516
```

## Loading AusTraits database 

You can load any version of AusTraits with `load_austraits`. By default, `load_austraits` will download AusTraits to a specified path e.g. `data/austraits` and will reload it from this location in subsequent sessions. `load_austraits` will also accept a DOI of a particular version.


```r
austraits_4.0.0 <- load_austraits(version = "4.0.0", path = "data/austraits")
```


In R, the AusTraits database arranged as a very *long* list with multiple of elements. If you are not familiar with working with lists in R, we recommend having a quick look at this [tutorial](https://www.tutorialspoint.com/r/r_lists.htm). To learn more about the structure of `austraits`, check out the [structure of the database](https://traitecoevo.github.io/austraits/articles/structure.html). 

Calling the AusTraits object will print out a quick summary of the database and meta-data descriptions of each table in the long list.


```r
austraits_4.0.0
```

```
#> This is version 4.0.0 of austraits!
#>  
#> This object contains a total of 1257443 records for 34028 taxa and 470 traits.
#> 
#> This object is a 'list' with the following components:
#> 
#>  - `traits`: A table containing measurements of traits.
#> - `locations`: A table containing observations of location/site characteristics associated with information in `traits`. Cross referencing between the two dataframes is possible using combinations of the variables `dataset_id`, `location_name`.
#> - `contexts`: A table containing observations of contextual characteristics associated with information in `traits`. Cross referencing between the two dataframes is possible using combinations of the variables `dataset_id`, `link_id`, and `link_vals`.
#> - `methods`: A table containing details on methods with which data were collected, including time frame and source. Cross referencing with the `traits` table is possible using combinations of the variables `dataset_id`, `trait_name`.
#> - `excluded_data`: A table of data that did not pass quality tests and so were excluded from the master dataset. The structure is identical to that presented in the `traits` table, only with an extra column called `error` indicating why the record was excluded. Common reasons are missing_unit_conversions, missing_value, and unsupported_trait_value.
#> - `taxonomic_updates`: A table of all taxonomic changes implemented in the construction of AusTraits. Changes are determined by comparing the originally submitted taxon name against the taxonomic names listed in the taxonomic reference files, best placed in a subfolder in the `config` folder . Cross referencing with the `traits` table is possible using combinations of the variables `dataset_id` and `taxon_name`.
#> - `taxa`: A table containing details on taxa associated with information in `traits`. Whenever possible, this information is sourced from curated taxon lists that include identifiers for each taxon. The information compiled in this table is released under a CC-BY3 license. Cross referencing between the two dataframes is possible using combinations of the variable `taxon_name`.
#> - `contributors`: A table of people contributing to each study.
#> - `sources`: Bibtex entries for all primary and secondary sources in the compilation.
#> - `definitions`: A copy of the definitions for all tables and terms. Information included here was used to process data and generate any documentation for the study.
#> - `schema`: A copy of the schema for all tables and terms. Information included here was used to process data and generate any documentation for the study.
#> - `metadata`: Metadata associated with the dataset, including title, creators, license, subject, funding sources.
#> - `build_info`: A description of the computing environment used to create this version of the dataset, including version number, git commit and R session_info.
#> 
#> To access a component, try using the $ e.g. austraits$traits
```

## Descriptive summaries of traits and taxa

AusTraits contains over 470 plant traits. Check out [definitions of the traits](https://traitecoevo.github.io/austraits/articles/dictionary.html) to learn more about how each trait is defined. 

Users can explore AusTraits and see how much data we have for certain traits or  taxonomic groups  using the `summarise_` functions

### Summarise by trait name


```r
summarise_austraits(austraits_4.0.0, "trait_name") %>% head()
```

```
#>                     trait_name n_records n_dataset n_taxa percent_total
#>        accessory_cost_fraction        47         1     47      3.74e-05
#>            accessory_cost_mass        47         1     47      3.74e-05
#>  atmospheric_CO2_concentration       785         3    113      6.24e-04
#>           bark_Al_per_dry_mass        70         1     10      5.57e-05
#>          bark_ash_per_dry_mass        63         1     10      5.01e-05
#>            bark_B_per_dry_mass        70         1     10      5.57e-05
```

### Summarise by taxonomic levels


```r
summarise_austraits(austraits_4.0.0, var =  "family") %>% head()
```

```
#>         family n_records n_dataset n_taxa percent_total
#>    Acanthaceae      2399        38    151      1.88e-03
#>    Achariaceae       104        11      4      8.15e-05
#>  Actinidiaceae        88        10      3      6.90e-05
#>  Agapanthaceae        47         6      3      3.68e-05
#>      Aizoaceae      2695        45    108      2.11e-03
#>     Akaniaceae        71        12      1      5.56e-05
```

```r
summarise_austraits(austraits_4.0.0, "genus") %>% head()
```

```
#>         genus n_records n_dataset n_taxa percent_total
#>   (Dockrillia         2         1      1      1.57e-06
#>        Abelia         6         2      1      4.70e-06
#>   Abelmoschus       107        10      8      8.37e-05
#>  Abildgaardia        65         5      3      5.09e-05
#>   Abrodictyum        65         7      3      5.09e-05
#>        Abroma        35         5      2      2.74e-05
```

## Search for trait

If you are interested in a specific trait? Try `lookup_trait`


```r
lookup_trait(austraits_4.0.0, "leaf") %>% head()
```

```
#> [1] "leaf_compoundness" "leaf_length"       "leaf_phenology"    "leaf_width"        "leaf_area"        
#> [6] "leaf_delta13C"
```

## Extract information from AusTraits 

The `extract_` functions can help users to filter down to the data the need. Users can extract data by their search results, the trait name, taxonomic information or by dataset.

### By search results

Combining `lookup_trait` with `extract_trait`, users can extract  **all traits with 'leaf' in the trait name**. Note we use the `.` notation to pass on the `lookup_trait` results to `extract_trait`


```r
data_leaf <- lookup_trait(austraits_4.0.0, "leaf") %>% extract_trait(austraits_4.0.0, .) 

data_leaf$traits
```

```
#> # A tibble: 330,591 × 24
#>    dataset_id taxon_name  obser…¹ trait…² value unit  entit…³ value…⁴ basis…⁵ repli…⁶ basis…⁷ life_…⁸ popul…⁹ indiv…˟
#>    <chr>      <chr>       <chr>   <chr>   <chr> <chr> <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>  
#>  1 ABRS_1981  Acanthocar… 0001    leaf_c… simp… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  2 ABRS_1981  Acanthocar… 0001    leaf_l… 3     mm    species minimum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  3 ABRS_1981  Acanthocar… 0001    leaf_l… 15    mm    species maximum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  4 ABRS_1981  Acanthocar… 0002    leaf_c… simp… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  5 ABRS_1981  Acanthocar… 0002    leaf_l… 4     mm    species minimum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  6 ABRS_1981  Acanthocar… 0002    leaf_l… 12    mm    species maximum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  7 ABRS_1981  Acanthocar… 0003    leaf_c… simp… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  8 ABRS_1981  Acanthocar… 0003    leaf_l… 3     mm    species minimum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  9 ABRS_1981  Acanthocar… 0004    leaf_c… simp… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#> 10 ABRS_1981  Acanthocar… 0004    leaf_l… 20    mm    species minimum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#> # … with 330,581 more rows, 10 more variables: temporal_id <chr>, source_id <chr>, location_id <chr>,
#> #   entity_context_id <chr>, plot_id <chr>, treatment_id <chr>, collection_date <chr>, measurement_remarks <chr>,
#> #   method_id <chr>, original_name <chr>, and abbreviated variable names ¹​observation_id, ²​trait_name, ³​entity_type,
#> #   ⁴​value_type, ⁵​basis_of_value, ⁶​replicates, ⁷​basis_of_record, ⁸​life_stage, ⁹​population_id, ˟​individual_id
```

### By trait name


```r
leafmass_data <- austraits_4.0.0 %>%
  extract_trait("leaf_mass_per_area") 
  
leafmass_data$trait
```

```
#> # A tibble: 27,949 × 24
#>    dataset_id  taxon_name obser…¹ trait…² value unit  entit…³ value…⁴ basis…⁵ repli…⁶ basis…⁷ life_…⁸ popul…⁹ indiv…˟
#>    <chr>       <chr>      <chr>   <chr>   <dbl> <chr> <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>  
#>  1 Ahrens_2019 Corymbia … 001     leaf_m…  185. g/m2  indivi… raw     measur… 1       field_… sapling 01      01     
#>  2 Ahrens_2019 Corymbia … 002     leaf_m…  138. g/m2  indivi… raw     measur… 1       field_… sapling 01      02     
#>  3 Ahrens_2019 Corymbia … 003     leaf_m…  169. g/m2  indivi… raw     measur… 1       field_… sapling 01      03     
#>  4 Ahrens_2019 Corymbia … 004     leaf_m…  151. g/m2  indivi… raw     measur… 1       field_… sapling 01      04     
#>  5 Ahrens_2019 Corymbia … 005     leaf_m…  142. g/m2  indivi… raw     measur… 1       field_… sapling 01      05     
#>  6 Ahrens_2019 Corymbia … 006     leaf_m…  156. g/m2  indivi… raw     measur… 1       field_… sapling 01      06     
#>  7 Ahrens_2019 Corymbia … 007     leaf_m…  150. g/m2  indivi… raw     measur… 1       field_… sapling 01      07     
#>  8 Ahrens_2019 Corymbia … 008     leaf_m…  175. g/m2  indivi… raw     measur… 1       field_… sapling 01      08     
#>  9 Ahrens_2019 Corymbia … 009     leaf_m…  148. g/m2  indivi… raw     measur… 1       field_… sapling 01      09     
#> 10 Ahrens_2019 Corymbia … 010     leaf_m…  135. g/m2  indivi… raw     measur… 1       field_… sapling 01      10     
#> # … with 27,939 more rows, 10 more variables: temporal_id <chr>, source_id <chr>, location_id <chr>,
#> #   entity_context_id <chr>, plot_id <chr>, treatment_id <chr>, collection_date <chr>, measurement_remarks <chr>,
#> #   method_id <chr>, original_name <chr>, and abbreviated variable names ¹​observation_id, ²​trait_name, ³​entity_type,
#> #   ⁴​value_type, ⁵​basis_of_value, ⁶​replicates, ⁷​basis_of_record, ⁸​life_stage, ⁹​population_id, ˟​individual_id
```

### By taxonomic information

#### By family

```r
proteaceae_data <- austraits_4.0.0 %>%
  extract_taxa(family = "Proteaceae")
  
proteaceae_data$trait
```

```
#> # A tibble: 91,221 × 24
#>    dataset_id taxon_name  obser…¹ trait…² value unit  entit…³ value…⁴ basis…⁵ repli…⁶ basis…⁷ life_…⁸ popul…⁹ indiv…˟
#>    <chr>      <chr>       <chr>   <chr>   <chr> <chr> <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>  
#>  1 ABRS_1981  Acidonia m… 0009    leaf_c… comp… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  2 ABRS_1981  Acidonia m… 0009    leaf_l… 60    mm    species minimum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  3 ABRS_1981  Acidonia m… 0009    leaf_l… 130   mm    species maximum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  4 ABRS_1981  Acidonia m… 0009    plant_… shrub <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  5 ABRS_1981  Acidonia m… 0009    plant_… 3     m     species maximum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  6 ABRS_1981  Adenanthos… 0015    leaf_c… simp… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  7 ABRS_1981  Adenanthos… 0015    leaf_l… 30    mm    species maximum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  8 ABRS_1981  Adenanthos… 0015    leaf_w… 30    mm    species maximum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  9 ABRS_1981  Adenanthos… 0015    plant_… shrub <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#> 10 ABRS_1981  Adenanthos… 0015    plant_… 5     m     species maximum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#> # … with 91,211 more rows, 10 more variables: temporal_id <chr>, source_id <chr>, location_id <chr>,
#> #   entity_context_id <chr>, plot_id <chr>, treatment_id <chr>, collection_date <chr>, measurement_remarks <chr>,
#> #   method_id <chr>, original_name <chr>, and abbreviated variable names ¹​observation_id, ²​trait_name, ³​entity_type,
#> #   ⁴​value_type, ⁵​basis_of_value, ⁶​replicates, ⁷​basis_of_record, ⁸​life_stage, ⁹​population_id, ˟​individual_id
```

#### By genus


```r
banksia_data <- austraits_4.0.0 %>%
  extract_taxa(genus = "Banksia")
  
banksia_data$trait
```

```
#> # A tibble: 16,991 × 24
#>    dataset_id taxon_name  obser…¹ trait…² value unit  entit…³ value…⁴ basis…⁵ repli…⁶ basis…⁷ life_…⁸ popul…⁹ indiv…˟
#>    <chr>      <chr>       <chr>   <chr>   <chr> <chr> <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>  
#>  1 ABRS_1981  Banksia ac… 0358    flower… nyyn… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  2 ABRS_1981  Banksia ac… 0358    leaf_c… simp… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  3 ABRS_1981  Banksia ac… 0358    leaf_l… 40    mm    species minimum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  4 ABRS_1981  Banksia ac… 0358    leaf_l… 90    mm    species maximum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  5 ABRS_1981  Banksia ac… 0358    leaf_p… ever… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  6 ABRS_1981  Banksia ac… 0358    leaf_w… 8     mm    species minimum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  7 ABRS_1981  Banksia ac… 0358    leaf_w… 30    mm    species maximum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  8 ABRS_1981  Banksia ac… 0358    life_h… pere… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  9 ABRS_1981  Banksia ac… 0358    plant_… shrub <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#> 10 ABRS_1981  Banksia ac… 0358    plant_… 2     m     species maximum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#> # … with 16,981 more rows, 10 more variables: temporal_id <chr>, source_id <chr>, location_id <chr>,
#> #   entity_context_id <chr>, plot_id <chr>, treatment_id <chr>, collection_date <chr>, measurement_remarks <chr>,
#> #   method_id <chr>, original_name <chr>, and abbreviated variable names ¹​observation_id, ²​trait_name, ³​entity_type,
#> #   ⁴​value_type, ⁵​basis_of_value, ⁶​replicates, ⁷​basis_of_record, ⁸​life_stage, ⁹​population_id, ˟​individual_id
```

#### By taxon name

```r
banksiaserrata_data <- austraits_4.0.0 %>%
  extract_taxa(taxon_name = "Banksia serrata") 
  
banksiaserrata_data$trait
```

```
#> # A tibble: 840 × 24
#>    dataset_id taxon_name  obser…¹ trait…² value unit  entit…³ value…⁴ basis…⁵ repli…⁶ basis…⁷ life_…⁸ popul…⁹ indiv…˟
#>    <chr>      <chr>       <chr>   <chr>   <chr> <chr> <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>  
#>  1 ABRS_1981  Banksia se… 0445    flower… yyyy… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  2 ABRS_1981  Banksia se… 0445    leaf_c… simp… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  3 ABRS_1981  Banksia se… 0445    leaf_l… 70    mm    species minimum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  4 ABRS_1981  Banksia se… 0445    leaf_l… 220   mm    species maximum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  5 ABRS_1981  Banksia se… 0445    leaf_p… ever… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  6 ABRS_1981  Banksia se… 0445    leaf_w… 20    mm    species minimum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  7 ABRS_1981  Banksia se… 0445    leaf_w… 40    mm    species maximum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  8 ABRS_1981  Banksia se… 0445    life_h… pere… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#>  9 ABRS_1981  Banksia se… 0445    plant_… shru… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#> 10 ABRS_1981  Banksia se… 0445    plant_… 16    m     species maximum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>   
#> # … with 830 more rows, 10 more variables: temporal_id <chr>, source_id <chr>, location_id <chr>,
#> #   entity_context_id <chr>, plot_id <chr>, treatment_id <chr>, collection_date <chr>, measurement_remarks <chr>,
#> #   method_id <chr>, original_name <chr>, and abbreviated variable names ¹​observation_id, ²​trait_name, ³​entity_type,
#> #   ⁴​value_type, ⁵​basis_of_value, ⁶​replicates, ⁷​basis_of_record, ⁸​life_stage, ⁹​population_id, ˟​individual_id
```

#### By dataset


```r
Westoby_2014 <- austraits_4.0.0 %>%
  extract_dataset("Westoby_2014") 
  
Westoby_2014$trait
```

```
#> # A tibble: 4,225 × 24
#>    dataset_id   taxon_n…¹ obser…² trait…³ value unit  entit…⁴ value…⁵ basis…⁶ repli…⁷ basis…⁸ life_…⁹ popul…˟ indiv…˟
#>    <chr>        <chr>     <chr>   <chr>   <chr> <chr> <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>  
#>  1 Westoby_2014 Acacia a… 001     leaf_a… 199.6 mm2   popula… mean    measur… 5       field   adult   04      <NA>   
#>  2 Westoby_2014 Acacia a… 001     leaf_h… 4.055 mmol… popula… maximum measur… 3       field   adult   04      <NA>   
#>  3 Westoby_2014 Acacia a… 001     leaf_m… 402.… g/m2  popula… mean    measur… 5       field   adult   04      <NA>   
#>  4 Westoby_2014 Acacia a… 001     leaf_s… 4.884 10^4… popula… mean    measur… 5       field   adult   04      <NA>   
#>  5 Westoby_2014 Acacia a… 001     water_… -3.0… MPa   popula… mean    measur… 3       field   adult   04      <NA>   
#>  6 Westoby_2014 Acacia a… 001     water_… -7.0… MPa   popula… mean    measur… 3       field   adult   04      <NA>   
#>  7 Westoby_2014 Acacia a… 001     wood_d… 0.837 mg/m… popula… mean    measur… 5       field   adult   04      <NA>   
#>  8 Westoby_2014 Acacia c… 002     wood_d… 0.945 mg/m… indivi… raw     measur… 1       field   adult   08      01     
#>  9 Westoby_2014 Acacia c… 003     wood_d… 0.80… mg/m… indivi… raw     measur… 1       field   adult   08      02     
#> 10 Westoby_2014 Acacia c… 004     wood_d… 0.80… mg/m… indivi… raw     measur… 1       field   adult   08      03     
#> # … with 4,215 more rows, 10 more variables: temporal_id <chr>, source_id <chr>, location_id <chr>,
#> #   entity_context_id <chr>, plot_id <chr>, treatment_id <chr>, collection_date <chr>, measurement_remarks <chr>,
#> #   method_id <chr>, original_name <chr>, and abbreviated variable names ¹​taxon_name, ²​observation_id, ³​trait_name,
#> #   ⁴​entity_type, ⁵​value_type, ⁶​basis_of_value, ⁷​replicates, ⁸​basis_of_record, ⁹​life_stage, ˟​population_id,
#> #   ˟​individual_id
```


## Joining information from other tables

AusTraits is a relational database. This means information are stored in different tables and are linked by certain meta-data columns such as `location_id`. Once users have extracted the data they want, they may want to merge other study details into the main `traits` table for their analyses. For example, users may require taxonomic information for a phylogenetic analysis. This is where the `join_` functions come in. 

There are five `join_` functions in total, each designed to append specific information from other tables in the `austraits` object. Their suffixes refer to the type of information that is joined, e.g. `join_taxonomy` appends taxonomic information. See `?join_all` for more details. 

#### Join taxonomic information 


```r
(data_leaf %>% join_taxonomy)$traits %>% head()
```

```
#> # A tibble: 6 × 28
#>   datas…¹ taxon…² obser…³ trait…⁴ value unit  entit…⁵ value…⁶ basis…⁷ repli…⁸ basis…⁹ life_…˟ popul…˟ indiv…˟ tempo…˟
#>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr> <chr> <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>  
#> 1 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0001    leaf_c… simp… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 2 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0001    leaf_l… 3     mm    species minimum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 3 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0001    leaf_l… 15    mm    species maximum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 4 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0002    leaf_c… simp… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 5 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0002    leaf_l… 4     mm    species minimum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 6 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0002    leaf_l… 12    mm    species maximum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> # … with 13 more variables: source_id <chr>, location_id <chr>, entity_context_id <chr>, plot_id <chr>,
#> #   treatment_id <chr>, collection_date <chr>, measurement_remarks <chr>, method_id <chr>, original_name <chr>,
#> #   family <chr>, genus <chr>, taxon_rank <chr>, establishment_means <chr>, and abbreviated variable names
#> #   ¹​dataset_id, ²​taxon_name, ³​observation_id, ⁴​trait_name, ⁵​entity_type, ⁶​value_type, ⁷​basis_of_value, ⁸​replicates,
#> #   ⁹​basis_of_record, ˟​life_stage, ˟​population_id, ˟​individual_id, ˟​temporal_id
```

#### Join methodological information 


```r
(data_leaf %>% join_methods)$traits %>% head()
```

```
#> # A tibble: 6 × 25
#>   datas…¹ taxon…² obser…³ trait…⁴ value unit  entit…⁵ value…⁶ basis…⁷ repli…⁸ basis…⁹ life_…˟ popul…˟ indiv…˟ tempo…˟
#>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr> <chr> <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>  
#> 1 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0001    leaf_c… simp… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 2 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0001    leaf_l… 3     mm    species minimum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 3 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0001    leaf_l… 15    mm    species maximum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 4 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0002    leaf_c… simp… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 5 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0002    leaf_l… 4     mm    species minimum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 6 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0002    leaf_l… 12    mm    species maximum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> # … with 10 more variables: source_id <chr>, location_id <chr>, entity_context_id <chr>, plot_id <chr>,
#> #   treatment_id <chr>, collection_date <chr>, measurement_remarks <chr>, method_id <chr>, original_name <chr>,
#> #   methods <chr>, and abbreviated variable names ¹​dataset_id, ²​taxon_name, ³​observation_id, ⁴​trait_name,
#> #   ⁵​entity_type, ⁶​value_type, ⁷​basis_of_value, ⁸​replicates, ⁹​basis_of_record, ˟​life_stage, ˟​population_id,
#> #   ˟​individual_id, ˟​temporal_id
```

#### Join study specific information 


```r
(data_leaf %>% join_contexts)$traits %>% head()
```

```
#> # A tibble: 6 × 63
#>   datas…¹ taxon…² obser…³ trait…⁴ value unit  entit…⁵ value…⁶ basis…⁷ repli…⁸ basis…⁹ life_…˟ popul…˟ indiv…˟ tempo…˟
#>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr> <chr> <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>  
#> 1 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0001    leaf_c… simp… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 2 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0001    leaf_l… 3     mm    species minimum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 3 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0001    leaf_l… 15    mm    species maximum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 4 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0002    leaf_c… simp… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 5 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0002    leaf_l… 4     mm    species minimum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 6 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0002    leaf_l… 12    mm    species maximum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> # … with 48 more variables: source_id <chr>, location_id <chr>, entity_context_id <chr>, plot_id <chr>,
#> #   treatment_id <chr>, collection_date <chr>, measurement_remarks <chr>, method_id <chr>, original_name <chr>,
#> #   `sampling season` <chr>, `measurement CO2` <chr>, `measurement irradiance` <chr>, `repeat observations` <chr>,
#> #   `fire history` <chr>, `replicate observations` <chr>, `canopy layer` <chr>, `drought treatment` <chr>,
#> #   `sampling time of day` <chr>, `temperature treatment` <chr>, `CO2 treatment` <chr>,
#> #   `measurement temperature` <chr>, plots <chr>, `leaf age` <chr>, blocks <chr>,
#> #   `drought treatment duration` <chr>, `seed age` <chr>, `sapwood sampling method` <chr>, …
```

#### Join location information 


```r
(data_leaf %>% join_locations)$traits %>% head()
```

```
#> # A tibble: 6 × 27
#>   datas…¹ taxon…² obser…³ trait…⁴ value unit  entit…⁵ value…⁶ basis…⁷ repli…⁸ basis…⁹ life_…˟ popul…˟ indiv…˟ tempo…˟
#>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr> <chr> <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>  
#> 1 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0001    leaf_c… simp… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 2 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0001    leaf_l… 3     mm    species minimum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 3 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0001    leaf_l… 15    mm    species maximum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 4 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0002    leaf_c… simp… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 5 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0002    leaf_l… 4     mm    species minimum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 6 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0002    leaf_l… 12    mm    species maximum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> # … with 12 more variables: source_id <chr>, location_id <chr>, entity_context_id <chr>, plot_id <chr>,
#> #   treatment_id <chr>, collection_date <chr>, measurement_remarks <chr>, method_id <chr>, original_name <chr>,
#> #   location_name <chr>, `latitude (deg)` <chr>, `longitude (deg)` <chr>, and abbreviated variable names
#> #   ¹​dataset_id, ²​taxon_name, ³​observation_id, ⁴​trait_name, ⁵​entity_type, ⁶​value_type, ⁷​basis_of_value, ⁸​replicates,
#> #   ⁹​basis_of_record, ˟​life_stage, ˟​population_id, ˟​individual_id, ˟​temporal_id
```

Alternatively users can join *all* information 

```r
(data_leaf %>% join_all)$traits %>% head()
```

```
#> # A tibble: 6 × 32
#>   datas…¹ taxon…² obser…³ trait…⁴ value unit  entit…⁵ value…⁶ basis…⁷ repli…⁸ basis…⁹ life_…˟ popul…˟ indiv…˟ tempo…˟
#>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr> <chr> <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>  
#> 1 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0001    leaf_c… simp… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 2 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0001    leaf_l… 3     mm    species minimum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 3 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0001    leaf_l… 15    mm    species maximum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 4 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0002    leaf_c… simp… <NA>  species mode    expert… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 5 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0002    leaf_l… 4     mm    species minimum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> 6 ABRS_1… Acanth… 0002    leaf_l… 12    mm    species maximum measur… <NA>    preser… adult   <NA>    <NA>    <NA>   
#> # … with 17 more variables: source_id <chr>, location_id <chr>, entity_context_id <chr>, plot_id <chr>,
#> #   treatment_id <chr>, collection_date <chr>, measurement_remarks <chr>, method_id <chr>, original_name <chr>,
#> #   location_name <chr>, `latitude (deg)` <chr>, `longitude (deg)` <chr>, family <chr>, genus <chr>,
#> #   taxon_rank <chr>, establishment_means <chr>, methods <chr>, and abbreviated variable names ¹​dataset_id,
#> #   ²​taxon_name, ³​observation_id, ⁴​trait_name, ⁵​entity_type, ⁶​value_type, ⁷​basis_of_value, ⁸​replicates,
#> #   ⁹​basis_of_record, ˟​life_stage, ˟​population_id, ˟​individual_id, ˟​temporal_id
```


## Transforming AusTraits into different formats 

The traits table in AusTraits is arranged in **long** format, where every observation is denoted by  meta-data columns such as `entity_type`, `value_type`, `replicates`, `population_id`, `individual_id`, `temporal_id`.

### Pivotting from 'long' to 'wide' format 
Users can convert this to wide format, where each trait is in a separate column using the function `trait_pivot_wider`. Note that this function will remove some meta-data columns during the pivoting process. 


```r
wide_Westoby_2014 <- Westoby_2014$traits %>% trait_pivot_wider() 
```

### Pivot longer

We can revert back to a wide format dataset using `trait_pivot_longer`, however this process is imperfect as observations can not be fully delineated without the meta-data columns that were removed during `trait_pivot_wider`


```r
wide_Westoby_2014 %>% trait_pivot_longer() 
```

```
#> # A tibble: 4,090 × 19
#> # Groups:   dataset_id, source_id, taxon_name, original_name, observation_id, method_id [516]
#>    dataset_id taxon…¹ obser…² entit…³ life_…⁴ popul…⁵ indiv…⁶ tempo…⁷ sourc…⁸ locat…⁹ entit…˟ plot_id treat…˟ colle…˟
#>    <chr>      <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>  
#>  1 Westoby_2… Acacia… 001     popula… adult   04      <NA>    <NA>    <NA>    04      <NA>    <NA>    <NA>    2011/2…
#>  2 Westoby_2… Acacia… 001     popula… adult   04      <NA>    <NA>    <NA>    04      <NA>    <NA>    <NA>    2011/2…
#>  3 Westoby_2… Acacia… 001     popula… adult   04      <NA>    <NA>    <NA>    04      <NA>    <NA>    <NA>    2011/2…
#>  4 Westoby_2… Acacia… 001     popula… adult   04      <NA>    <NA>    <NA>    04      <NA>    <NA>    <NA>    2011/2…
#>  5 Westoby_2… Acacia… 001     popula… adult   04      <NA>    <NA>    <NA>    04      <NA>    <NA>    <NA>    2011/2…
#>  6 Westoby_2… Acacia… 001     popula… adult   04      <NA>    <NA>    <NA>    04      <NA>    <NA>    <NA>    2011/2…
#>  7 Westoby_2… Acacia… 002     indivi… adult   08      01      <NA>    <NA>    08      <NA>    <NA>    <NA>    2011/2…
#>  8 Westoby_2… Acacia… 003     indivi… adult   08      02      <NA>    <NA>    08      <NA>    <NA>    <NA>    2011/2…
#>  9 Westoby_2… Acacia… 004     indivi… adult   08      03      <NA>    <NA>    08      <NA>    <NA>    <NA>    2011/2…
#> 10 Westoby_2… Acacia… 005     popula… adult   08      <NA>    <NA>    <NA>    08      <NA>    <NA>    <NA>    2011/2…
#> # … with 4,080 more rows, 5 more variables: method_id <chr>, original_name <chr>, leaf_area <chr>, trait_name <chr>,
#> #   value <chr>, and abbreviated variable names ¹​taxon_name, ²​observation_id, ³​entity_type, ⁴​life_stage,
#> #   ⁵​population_id, ⁶​individual_id, ⁷​temporal_id, ⁸​source_id, ⁹​location_id, ˟​entity_context_id, ˟​treatment_id,
#> #   ˟​collection_date
```

### Transform into a single table

Users can also transform the entire relational database into one wide table. This approach turns the list structure into one single dataframe. 


```r
Westoby_2014 %>% as_wide_table()
```

```
#> # A tibble: 4,259 × 53
#>    dataset_id   taxon_n…¹ obser…² trait…³ value unit  entit…⁴ value…⁵ basis…⁶ repli…⁷ basis…⁸ life_…⁹ popul…˟ indiv…˟
#>    <chr>        <chr>     <chr>   <chr>   <chr> <chr> <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>  
#>  1 Westoby_2014 Acacia a… 001     leaf_a… 199.6 mm2   popula… mean    measur… 5       field   adult   04      <NA>   
#>  2 Westoby_2014 Acacia a… 001     leaf_h… 4.055 mmol… popula… maximum measur… 3       field   adult   04      <NA>   
#>  3 Westoby_2014 Acacia a… 001     leaf_m… 402.… g/m2  popula… mean    measur… 5       field   adult   04      <NA>   
#>  4 Westoby_2014 Acacia a… 001     leaf_s… 4.884 10^4… popula… mean    measur… 5       field   adult   04      <NA>   
#>  5 Westoby_2014 Acacia a… 001     water_… -3.0… MPa   popula… mean    measur… 3       field   adult   04      <NA>   
#>  6 Westoby_2014 Acacia a… 001     water_… -7.0… MPa   popula… mean    measur… 3       field   adult   04      <NA>   
#>  7 Westoby_2014 Acacia a… 001     wood_d… 0.837 mg/m… popula… mean    measur… 5       field   adult   04      <NA>   
#>  8 Westoby_2014 Acacia c… 002     wood_d… 0.945 mg/m… indivi… raw     measur… 1       field   adult   08      01     
#>  9 Westoby_2014 Acacia c… 003     wood_d… 0.80… mg/m… indivi… raw     measur… 1       field   adult   08      02     
#> 10 Westoby_2014 Acacia c… 004     wood_d… 0.80… mg/m… indivi… raw     measur… 1       field   adult   08      03     
#> # … with 4,249 more rows, 39 more variables: temporal_id <chr>, source_id <chr>, location_id <chr>,
#> #   entity_context_id <chr>, plot_id <chr>, treatment_id <chr>, collection_date <chr>, measurement_remarks <chr>,
#> #   method_id <chr>, original_name <chr>, context <chr>, `latitude (deg)` <chr>, `longitude (deg)` <chr>,
#> #   location <chr>, methods <chr>, dataset_description <chr>, sampling_strategy <chr>, source_primary_key <chr>,
#> #   source_primary_citation <chr>, source_secondary_key <chr>, source_secondary_citation <chr>,
#> #   source_original_dataset_key <chr>, source_original_dataset_citation <chr>, data_collectors <chr>,
#> #   assistants <chr>, austraits_curators <chr>, taxonNameValidation <chr>, taxon_rank <chr>, trinomial <chr>, …
```

## Visualise AusTraits data

### Spatial distribution of trait data

`plot_locations` graphically summarises where trait data was collected from and how much data is available for a particular trait. The legend refers to the number of neighboring points: the warmer the colour, the more data is available. This function only works for studies that are geo-referenced. Users must first use `join_sites` to append latitude and longitude information into the traits table before plotting


```r
wood_density_data <- austraits_4.0.0 %>% 
  extract_trait("wood_density") %>% 
  join_locations()
  
wood_density_data %>% 
  plot_locations(feature = "trait_name")
```

<img src="man/figures/unnamed-chunk-23-1.png" alt="plot of chunk unnamed-chunk-23" style="display: block; margin: auto;" />

### Beeswarm distribution of trait data

`plot_trait_distribution` creates histograms and [beeswarm plots](https://github.com/eclarke/ggbeeswarm) for specific traits to help users visualise the variance of the data. Users can specify whether to create separate beeswarm plots at the level of taxonomic family or for each `dataset_id` to compare variance between studies.


```r
banksiaserrata_data %>% 
  plot_trait_distribution_beeswarm("leaf_area", "dataset_id", highlight="Westoby_2014", hide_ids = TRUE) 
```

<img src="man/figures/unnamed-chunk-24-1.png" alt="plot of chunk unnamed-chunk-24" style="display: block; margin: auto;" />
